title: Mutual Confidentiality
body: Each party (the "Receiving Party") acknowledges that in the course of performing this Agreement it may receive Confidential Information of the other party (the "Disclosing Party"). "Confidential Information" means any non-public information that is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure or that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure, including business plans, customer lists, pricing, source code, technical specifications, and personal data. The Receiving Party shall: (a) use the Confidential Information solely to perform its obligations under this Agreement; (b) protect it with at least the same degree of care it uses for its own confidential information of a similar nature, and in no event less than a reasonable standard of care; and (c) disclose it only to its personnel and advisors who need to know and who are bound by written obligations of confidentiality no less protective than those contained herein. This obligation shall survive termination of this Agreement for three (3) years, except for trade secrets and personal data, which shall be protected for as long as they retain their status under applicable law.

title: One-Way Confidentiality
body: The Recipient shall treat all Confidential Information disclosed by the Discloser as strictly confidential and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Discloser, disclose, publish, or use such Confidential Information for any purpose other than evaluating or furthering the Purpose set out in the preamble of this Agreement. The Recipient shall take all reasonable precautions to prevent unauthorised access to or use of the Confidential Information and shall be liable for any breach by its employees, agents, or contractors.

title: Exclusions from Confidentiality
body: The confidentiality obligations in this Agreement shall not apply to information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (b) was lawfully in the possession of the Receiving Party free of any obligation of confidence prior to disclosure by the Disclosing Party, as evidenced by its written records; (c) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to or use of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, as evidenced by its written records; or (d) is lawfully obtained by the Receiving Party from a third party who had the right to disclose it.

title: Compelled Disclosure
body: If the Receiving Party is compelled by law, court order, or the order of any governmental or regulatory authority to disclose any Confidential Information, it shall, to the extent legally permitted, provide the Disclosing Party with prompt prior written notice so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. The Receiving Party shall disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is legally required to disclose and shall use reasonable efforts to obtain confidential treatment of such Confidential Information.

title: Mutual Indemnification
body: Each party (the "Indemnifying Party") shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the other party and its affiliates, officers, directors, and employees (the "Indemnified Parties") from and against any third-party claims, damages, losses, liabilities, and reasonable expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or relating to (a) the Indemnifying Party's breach of its representations, warranties, or obligations under this Agreement; (b) the gross negligence or wilful misconduct of the Indemnifying Party; or (c) any violation of applicable law by the Indemnifying Party. The Indemnified Party shall (i) promptly notify the Indemnifying Party in writing of the claim, (ii) grant the Indemnifying Party sole control of the defence and settlement, provided that no settlement shall impose any admission of liability or non-monetary obligation on the Indemnified Party without its prior written consent, and (iii) provide reasonable assistance at the Indemnifying Party's expense.

title: Supplier IP Infringement Indemnity
body: The Supplier shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the Customer from any third-party claim alleging that the Services or the Software, when used by the Customer in accordance with this Agreement, infringe any patent, copyright, trade mark, or trade secret of such third party. If the use of the Services or the Software is enjoined or, in the Supplier's reasonable opinion, is likely to become the subject of such a claim, the Supplier shall, at its option and expense: (a) procure for the Customer the right to continue using the Services or the Software; (b) modify the Services or the Software so that they no longer infringe, without materially reducing the functionality; or (c) replace the Services or the Software with non-infringing functional equivalents. If none of the foregoing options is commercially reasonable, either party may terminate the affected portion of this Agreement and the Supplier shall refund any prepaid, unused fees. This indemnity does not apply to claims arising from (i) modifications not made by the Supplier, (ii) use in combination with third-party products not supplied by the Supplier, or (iii) use outside the scope of this Agreement.

title: Overall Liability Cap
body: Except for the Excluded Liabilities set out below, each party's total aggregate liability arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, whether in contract, tort (including negligence), strict liability, or otherwise, shall not exceed the total fees paid or payable by the Customer to the Supplier under this Agreement in the twelve (12) months immediately preceding the event giving rise to the liability. This limitation shall apply even if any remedy fails of its essential purpose.

title: Exclusion of Consequential Damages
body: Neither party shall be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, punitive, or exemplary damages, including without limitation loss of profits, loss of revenue, loss of business, loss of goodwill, or loss of anticipated savings, arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, even if such party has been advised of the possibility of such damages. This exclusion shall not apply to damages arising from a party's wilful misconduct, breach of confidentiality, or indemnification obligations under this Agreement.

title: Excluded Liabilities (Carve-outs)
body: The limitations and exclusions of liability in this Agreement shall not apply to: (a) either party's indemnification obligations; (b) breach of confidentiality obligations; (c) infringement of the other party's intellectual property rights; (d) a party's gross negligence, fraud, or wilful misconduct; (e) liability for death or personal injury caused by a party's negligence; (f) payment obligations; and (g) any other liability which cannot be excluded or limited under applicable law.

title: Termination for Convenience
body: Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon not less than thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other party. Upon termination under this clause, the Customer shall pay the Supplier for all Services properly rendered and fees accrued up to the effective date of termination, and neither party shall have any further liability to the other on account of such termination, save for accrued rights and obligations expressly surviving termination.

title: Termination for Cause
body: Either party may terminate this Agreement with immediate effect by written notice to the other party if: (a) the other party commits a material breach of this Agreement and, where such breach is capable of remedy, fails to remedy it within thirty (30) days after receiving written notice of the breach; (b) the other party becomes insolvent, enters into liquidation (other than for the purpose of solvent reconstruction), has a receiver or administrator appointed over its assets, or ceases or threatens to cease carrying on business; or (c) the other party is subject to a change of control to a direct competitor of the terminating party.

title: Effects of Termination
body: Upon termination or expiry of this Agreement, however caused: (a) all licences granted under this Agreement shall immediately cease; (b) each party shall return or, at the Disclosing Party's option, destroy all Confidential Information of the other party in its possession, and certify such destruction in writing on request; (c) the Customer shall pay all fees accrued up to the effective date of termination; (d) any provisions of this Agreement which by their nature are intended to survive, including those relating to confidentiality, intellectual property, limitation of liability, indemnification, governing law, and dispute resolution, shall survive such termination; and (e) termination shall not affect any rights or remedies that have accrued prior to such termination.

title: DPDPA 2023 Data Processing Clause
body: Where the Supplier processes Personal Data (as defined under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 of India) on behalf of the Customer in the course of providing the Services, the Supplier shall act as a Data Processor and the Customer as the Data Fiduciary. The Supplier shall: (a) process Personal Data only on documented, written instructions from the Customer and only to the extent necessary to provide the Services; (b) implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect Personal Data against unauthorised or unlawful processing, accidental loss, destruction, or damage, in accordance with Section 8 of the DPDPA; (c) ensure that persons authorised to process the Personal Data are bound by appropriate confidentiality obligations; (d) not engage any sub-processor without the Customer's prior written consent and shall remain liable for the acts and omissions of any approved sub-processor; (e) assist the Customer in responding to requests from Data Principals exercising their rights under Chapter III of the DPDPA; (f) notify the Customer without undue delay, and in any event within seventy-two (72) hours, of any Personal Data breach affecting the Customer's data; and (g) on termination of the Services, return or securely delete all Personal Data as directed by the Customer, except to the extent retention is required by applicable law.

title: GDPR Article 28 Data Processing Clause
body: Where the Processor processes Personal Data on behalf of the Controller, the Processor shall comply with Article 28 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 ("GDPR") and, in particular, shall: (a) process Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a third country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; (b) ensure that persons authorised to process the Personal Data have committed themselves to confidentiality or are under an appropriate statutory obligation of confidentiality; (c) take all measures required pursuant to Article 32 GDPR; (d) respect the conditions referred to in Articles 28(2) and 28(4) for engaging another processor; (e) taking into account the nature of the processing, assist the Controller by appropriate technical and organisational measures, insofar as this is possible, for the fulfilment of the Controller's obligation to respond to requests for exercising the Data Subject's rights laid down in Chapter III GDPR; (f) assist the Controller in ensuring compliance with the obligations pursuant to Articles 32 to 36 GDPR taking into account the nature of processing and the information available to the Processor; (g) at the choice of the Controller, delete or return all the Personal Data to the Controller after the end of the provision of services relating to processing, and delete existing copies unless Union or Member State law requires storage of the Personal Data; and (h) make available to the Controller all information necessary to demonstrate compliance with the obligations laid down in this Article and allow for and contribute to audits, including inspections, conducted by the Controller or another auditor mandated by the Controller.

title: Information Security Measures
body: The Supplier shall implement and maintain technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risks presented by the processing of Personal Data, including as appropriate: (a) the pseudonymisation and encryption of Personal Data in transit and at rest; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of processing systems and services; (c) the ability to restore the availability of and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident; (d) a process for regularly testing, assessing, and evaluating the effectiveness of the measures; (e) role-based access controls and the principle of least privilege; (f) secure development practices aligned with OWASP ASVS or an equivalent standard; and (g) regular independent audits, the results of which shall be shared with the Customer on reasonable written request.

title: Personal Data Breach Notification
body: The Processor shall notify the Controller without undue delay, and in any event within seventy-two (72) hours, after becoming aware of a Personal Data Breach affecting the Controller's Personal Data. The notification shall: (a) describe the nature of the Personal Data Breach, including where possible the categories and approximate number of Data Subjects and records concerned; (b) communicate the name and contact details of the Data Protection Officer or other point of contact; (c) describe the likely consequences of the Personal Data Breach; and (d) describe the measures taken or proposed to be taken to address the Personal Data Breach, including measures to mitigate its possible adverse effects. The Processor shall provide all reasonable assistance to the Controller in fulfilling its obligations under Articles 33 and 34 GDPR or equivalent provisions under applicable data protection law, including the DPDPA 2023.

title: Sub-processor Appointment
body: The Processor shall not appoint any sub-processor to process Personal Data without the prior written authorisation of the Controller. The Processor shall maintain an up-to-date list of authorised sub-processors, including the name, location, and activities of each, and shall make such list available to the Controller on request. The Processor shall impose on each sub-processor, by way of a written contract, the same data protection obligations as are imposed on the Processor under this Agreement. The Processor shall give the Controller at least thirty (30) days' prior written notice of any intended change concerning the addition or replacement of sub-processors, during which the Controller may object on reasonable grounds. Where the Controller objects, the parties shall work in good faith to resolve the objection; failing which, the Controller may terminate the affected Services with a pro-rata refund of any prepaid, unused fees.

title: Ownership of Pre-existing IP
body: Each party shall retain all right, title, and interest in and to its pre-existing intellectual property, including any intellectual property developed outside of or independently from this Agreement. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as an assignment or transfer of such pre-existing intellectual property, save to the extent expressly licensed under this Agreement.

title: Assignment of Work Product
body: All work product, deliverables, documentation, and other materials created by the Supplier specifically for the Customer under this Agreement (the "Work Product") shall be the sole and exclusive property of the Customer. The Supplier hereby assigns and agrees to assign to the Customer all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product, including all associated intellectual property rights, on a worldwide, perpetual, irrevocable basis. The Supplier shall execute all documents and take all actions reasonably requested by the Customer to perfect, record, or enforce the Customer's rights in the Work Product. The Work Product shall exclude any pre-existing intellectual property or generally applicable tools, libraries, or methodologies of the Supplier, which shall remain the property of the Supplier; provided that the Supplier hereby grants the Customer a perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide, royalty-free, non-exclusive licence to use such pre-existing intellectual property solely to the extent necessary to use the Work Product for its intended purpose.

title: SaaS Licence Grant
body: Subject to the Customer's payment of all applicable fees and compliance with this Agreement, the Supplier grants the Customer a non-exclusive, non-transferable, non-sublicensable, worldwide right during the Subscription Term to access and use the Services and the Documentation solely for the Customer's internal business operations and only up to the number of Authorised Users and within the usage metrics set out in the applicable Order Form. The Customer shall not, and shall not permit any third party to: (a) reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble the Services, except to the extent such restriction is prohibited by applicable law; (b) modify, adapt, or create derivative works of the Services; (c) sell, resell, licence, sublicense, distribute, or make available the Services to any third party; (d) use the Services to build a competitive product or service; (e) remove or obscure any proprietary notices; or (f) use the Services in violation of applicable law.

title: Arbitration (India, Act of 1996)
body: Any dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, including any question regarding its existence, validity, or termination, shall be referred to and finally resolved by arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, as amended from time to time. The arbitral tribunal shall consist of a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by the parties; if the parties fail to agree within thirty (30) days, the arbitrator shall be appointed in accordance with the said Act. The seat and venue of arbitration shall be [●], India, and the language of the arbitration shall be English. The arbitral award shall be final and binding on the parties. Nothing in this clause shall preclude either party from seeking urgent interim or injunctive relief from any court of competent jurisdiction.

title: Pre-Arbitration Mediation
body: Before resorting to arbitration or litigation, the parties shall attempt in good faith to resolve any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement through mediation. A party wishing to initiate mediation shall give written notice to the other party, and the parties shall appoint a mutually acceptable mediator within fifteen (15) days of such notice. If the parties are unable to agree on a mediator, or if the dispute is not resolved within forty-five (45) days from the date of the mediation notice, either party may initiate arbitration or litigation in accordance with this Agreement. The costs of mediation shall be borne equally by the parties.

title: Governing Law and Jurisdiction (India)
body: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, without regard to its conflict of laws principles. Subject to any arbitration provisions in this Agreement, the courts at [●], India shall have exclusive jurisdiction over any matter arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.

title: Force Majeure Clause
body: Neither party shall be liable for any failure or delay in performing its obligations under this Agreement (other than payment obligations) to the extent that such failure or delay is caused by a Force Majeure Event. A "Force Majeure Event" means any event or circumstance beyond the reasonable control of the affected party, including acts of God, war, terrorism, riots, civil unrest, government action, embargoes, pandemic, epidemic, fire, flood, earthquake, or failures of telecommunications or utilities infrastructure, provided that the lack of funds or general economic conditions shall not constitute a Force Majeure Event. The affected party shall promptly notify the other party in writing of the Force Majeure Event and shall use reasonable efforts to mitigate its effects and resume performance. If a Force Majeure Event continues for more than sixty (60) days, either party may terminate this Agreement by written notice, without further liability save for accrued rights and obligations.

title: Mutual Warranty of Authority
body: Each party represents and warrants to the other that: (a) it is duly organised, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of its jurisdiction of incorporation; (b) it has full corporate power and authority to enter into and perform this Agreement, and has taken all necessary corporate action to authorise the execution and performance of this Agreement; (c) the person executing this Agreement on its behalf has been duly authorised to do so; and (d) this Agreement, when executed, will constitute a legal, valid, and binding obligation, enforceable against it in accordance with its terms.

title: Services Warranty
body: The Supplier warrants that: (a) the Services shall be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner, in accordance with generally accepted industry standards and the specifications set out in the applicable Order Form or Statement of Work; (b) it has and will maintain all necessary licences, permits, and authorisations to provide the Services; (c) the Services and any Deliverables shall not, to the Supplier's knowledge, infringe the intellectual property rights of any third party; and (d) the Supplier shall comply with all applicable laws in the performance of the Services. The Customer's sole and exclusive remedy for breach of this warranty shall be, at the Supplier's option, re-performance of the defective Services or refund of the fees paid for the defective Services, provided that the Customer notifies the Supplier of the breach within thirty (30) days of discovery.

title: Disclaimer of Implied Warranties
body: Except for the express warranties set out in this Agreement, to the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, the Supplier disclaims all other warranties, whether express, implied, statutory, or otherwise, including without limitation any implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, title, non-infringement, and any warranties arising from course of dealing or course of performance. The Supplier does not warrant that the Services will be uninterrupted, error-free, or completely secure, or that all errors or defects will be corrected.

title: Payment Terms (Net 30)
body: The Supplier shall invoice the Customer for fees in accordance with the applicable Order Form. All invoices are due and payable within thirty (30) days of the date of the invoice, without set-off or deduction, except for amounts disputed in good faith. Disputed amounts must be notified in writing within fifteen (15) days of the invoice date, specifying the reasons for the dispute; undisputed portions shall be paid when due. All amounts are exclusive of applicable taxes, including GST, which shall be borne by the Customer, except for taxes based on the Supplier's net income. Late payments shall accrue interest at the rate of one and a half per cent (1.5%) per month, or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is lower, from the due date until paid in full.

title: Taxes
body: All fees and charges under this Agreement are exclusive of all applicable taxes, levies, duties, and cesses, including Goods and Services Tax (GST) in India, which shall be charged in addition at the prevailing rate and paid by the Customer. Each party shall be responsible for its own income taxes. The Customer shall withhold tax at source at the rate prescribed by applicable law and remit the net amount to the Supplier, provided that the Customer issues a valid TDS certificate to the Supplier within the prescribed timelines. Where a double taxation avoidance treaty is applicable, the Supplier shall provide a valid Tax Residency Certificate to enable the Customer to apply the reduced rate.

title: Uptime Service Level (99.9%)
body: The Supplier shall use commercially reasonable efforts to make the Services available with a monthly uptime of at least ninety-nine decimal nine per cent (99.9%), calculated as the total number of minutes in the calendar month minus Excluded Downtime, divided by the total number of minutes in the calendar month, expressed as a percentage. "Excluded Downtime" means downtime caused by: (a) scheduled maintenance with at least seventy-two (72) hours' prior notice; (b) emergency maintenance required to address a security vulnerability; (c) Force Majeure Events; (d) any act or omission of the Customer or its users, including misuse of the Services; or (e) issues with third-party systems or networks outside the Supplier's reasonable control. Where the Services fail to meet the monthly uptime target, the Customer's sole and exclusive remedy shall be a service credit calculated as a percentage of the monthly fees for the affected Services, as set out in Schedule [●]. Service credits shall not exceed twenty-five per cent (25%) of the monthly fees.

title: Support Response Times
body: The Supplier shall provide support during Business Hours and respond to support requests in accordance with the following severity levels: Severity 1 (Critical — Services unavailable or major functionality unusable, no workaround): response within one (1) hour, continuous effort until resolution. Severity 2 (Major — significant degradation of functionality, workaround available): response within four (4) Business Hours, resolution target of two (2) Business Days. Severity 3 (Minor — non-critical issues, cosmetic or documentation): response within one (1) Business Day, resolution in the next reasonable release cycle. "Business Hours" means 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM IST on Business Days (Monday to Friday, excluding Indian public holidays).

title: Restriction on Assignment
body: Neither party may assign, transfer, or delegate any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement, whether by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other party, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may assign this Agreement in its entirety, without the other party's consent, to an affiliate or in connection with a merger, acquisition, corporate reorganisation, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets, provided that the assignee assumes all of the assigning party's obligations under this Agreement and the assigning party gives prompt written notice to the other party. Any attempted assignment in violation of this clause shall be null and void.

title: Governing Law — India
body: This Agreement and any dispute or claim (including non-contractual disputes or claims) arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter or formation shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, without giving effect to any choice or conflict of law provision.

title: Governing Law — England and Wales
body: This Agreement and any dispute or claim (including non-contractual disputes or claims) arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter or formation shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales. The courts of England shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.

title: Governing Law — Delaware, USA
body: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, United States of America, without regard to its conflict of laws principles. The parties consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in Wilmington, Delaware, for any action arising out of or relating to this Agreement.

title: Non-Solicitation of Personnel
body: During the Term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months thereafter, neither party shall directly or indirectly solicit for employment or engagement, or hire, any person who is or was an employee, contractor, or consultant of the other party and who was directly involved in the performance of this Agreement, without the prior written consent of the other party. The foregoing shall not apply to: (a) general solicitations not specifically directed at such individuals (including recruitment advertisements and postings); or (b) persons who approach the soliciting party on their own initiative without solicitation.

title: Notices
body: All notices, requests, consents, and other communications required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered: (a) by hand, with written confirmation of receipt; (b) by recognised courier service, with written confirmation of receipt; or (c) by email, with confirmation of transmission, followed by a hard copy sent by courier. Notices shall be sent to the address set out in the preamble of this Agreement, or to such other address as a party may specify from time to time by notice given in accordance with this clause. Notices shall be deemed effective: (i) on delivery, if delivered by hand or courier during Business Hours; (ii) on the next Business Day, if delivered after Business Hours; and (iii) on confirmation of transmission, if sent by email, provided receipt during Business Hours.

title: Amendments in Writing
body: No amendment, modification, or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by an authorised representative of each party. No waiver of any breach of any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any prior, concurrent, or subsequent breach of the same or any other provision of this Agreement.

title: Severability
body: If any provision of this Agreement is held by a court of competent jurisdiction to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, such provision shall be severed from this Agreement and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect. The parties shall negotiate in good faith to replace the invalid provision with a valid and enforceable provision that most closely reflects the parties' original intent.

title: Entire Agreement
body: This Agreement, together with any Order Forms, Statements of Work, and exhibits referenced herein, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written, between the parties relating to such subject matter. In the event of any conflict between this Agreement and any Order Form or Statement of Work, this Agreement shall prevail unless the Order Form or Statement of Work expressly states that it overrides a specific provision of this Agreement.

title: Counterparts and Electronic Signatures
body: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which, when executed, shall be an original, and all the counterparts together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Signatures delivered by electronic means (including by email in PDF format or through electronic signature services such as DocuSign or Adobe Sign) shall have the same legal effect as original handwritten signatures. The parties agree that the execution of this Agreement using electronic signatures complies with the Information Technology Act, 2000 of India, and any equivalent applicable law.

title: No Third-Party Beneficiaries
body: Except as expressly provided in this Agreement, nothing in this Agreement, express or implied, is intended to or shall confer on any person other than the parties and their respective permitted successors and assigns any right, benefit, or remedy of any nature whatsoever under or by reason of this Agreement.

title: Independent Contractors
body: The parties are independent contractors. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create a partnership, joint venture, employment, franchise, or agency relationship between the parties. Neither party shall have the authority to bind the other, to incur any obligation on the other's behalf, or to make any representation or warranty on the other's behalf, without the prior written consent of the other party.

title: Insurance Requirements
body: The Supplier shall, at its own expense, maintain during the Term of this Agreement and for a period of two (2) years thereafter, insurance policies of the following types and minimum amounts: (a) Commercial General Liability insurance with a combined single limit of not less than USD 1,000,000 per occurrence; (b) Professional Indemnity / Errors and Omissions insurance with a limit of not less than USD 2,000,000 per claim and in the aggregate, covering acts, errors, and omissions in the performance of the Services; (c) Cyber Liability insurance with a limit of not less than USD 1,000,000, covering data breach response, notification costs, and third-party liability for the loss of Personal Data; and (d) Workers' compensation insurance as required by applicable law. The Supplier shall furnish certificates of insurance to the Customer on request and shall give at least thirty (30) days' prior written notice of any cancellation or material change in coverage.

title: Audit Rights
body: The Supplier shall maintain complete and accurate records in relation to its performance of this Agreement for a period of not less than five (5) years after the expiry or termination of this Agreement. The Customer, or an independent auditor appointed by the Customer and reasonably acceptable to the Supplier, may, upon not less than fifteen (15) Business Days' prior written notice, audit such records to verify the Supplier's compliance with this Agreement. Audits shall be conducted during Business Hours, not more than once in any twelve (12) month period (except where a material breach has been identified), and in a manner that minimises disruption to the Supplier's business. The Customer shall bear the costs of the audit, unless the audit reveals a material breach by the Supplier, in which case the Supplier shall reimburse the Customer's reasonable audit costs within thirty (30) days of receipt of the audit report.

title: Publicity Restrictions
body: Neither party shall issue any press release, public statement, or marketing material referencing the other party, the existence of this Agreement, or its terms, without the prior written consent of the other party, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Supplier may include the Customer's name and logo in a list of customers on its website and in standard marketing materials, subject to the Customer's reasonable brand guidelines, and may disclose the existence of this Agreement to the extent required by applicable law, regulation, or the rules of any stock exchange on which the Supplier's securities are listed.
